name: Dodi Fayed Death
description: The death of Dodi Fayed, son of Egyptian businessman Mohamed Al-Fayed, alongside Princess Diana in a car crash in Paris on August 31, 1997, has been the subject of numerous conspiracy theories. These theories often allege that the crash was not an accident but a deliberate act orchestrated by the British establishment, specifically MI6, on the orders of the Royal Family or other powerful figures. Proponents suggest that Diana's relationship with Fayed, a Muslim, was deemed unacceptable, posing a threat to the monarchy. Some theories also involve the alleged pregnancy of Princess Diana. Key evidence cited by proponents includes discrepancies in witness testimonies, alleged tampering with the wreckage of the car, claims of suspicious activities by security services, and the perceived lack of a thorough investigation. Mainstream investigations, including the French investigation and the British Operation Paget led by Lord Stevens, concluded that the crash was caused by the reckless driving of Henri Paul, the deputy head of security at the Hôtel Ritz Paris, who was under the influence of alcohol and prescription drugs, combined with the aggressive pursuit by paparazzi. These investigations found no evidence of a conspiracy. Criticisms of the conspiracy theories often point to the lack of credible evidence, the reliance on unsubstantiated claims, and the tendency to selectively interpret information to fit a pre-determined narrative. The official reports are considered definitive by most mainstream media and legal experts, although Al-Fayed continued to dispute the findings until his death.
subtitle: Conspiracy surrounding Diana's death.

claims.claim: The crash was orchestrated by MI6 on the orders of the Royal Family.
claims.evidence: Alleged discrepancies in witness testimonies, suspicious activities by security services, and perceived motive.
claims.criticism: The official investigations found no evidence of MI6 involvement or Royal Family orders. Witness discrepancies are common in traumatic events and can be explained by human error and perception bias.

claims.claim: Princess Diana was pregnant with Dodi Fayed's child.
claims.evidence: Claims from Al-Fayed and reports of Diana seeking medical advice before the crash.
claims.criticism: The official investigations, including a post-mortem examination, found no evidence of pregnancy. Claims of medical appointments are unsubstantiated.

claims.claim: The car was deliberately tampered with before the crash.
claims.evidence: Allegations of sabotage and manipulation of the vehicle's braking system.
claims.criticism: Forensic investigations of the car found no evidence of tampering. The crash was attributed to the driver's intoxication and excessive speed.

keyFigures.name: Mohamed Al-Fayed
keyFigures.role: Promoter of conspiracy theories and father of Dodi Fayed

keyFigures.name: Lord Stevens
keyFigures.role: Head of Operation Paget, the British inquiry into the crash

keyFigures.name: John Macnamara
keyFigures.role: Former Head of Security at Harrods, investigated the crash for Al-Fayed

timeline.event: Dodi Fayed and Princess Diana die in a car crash in Paris.
French investigation concludes the crash was caused by Henri Paul's negligence.
Operation Paget, the British inquiry, investigates the conspiracy theories.
Inquest into Diana and Dodi's deaths concludes they were unlawfully killed due to the negligence of Henri Paul and the paparazzi.

sources.author: Colin McLaren
sources.work: "Diana and the Driver: The Truth About the Crash"
sources.title: Colin McLaren
